key_insights.insight: Monetary rewards can diminish intrinsic motivation for activities one initially enjoys.
key_insights.quote: You lose internal motivation when it becomes when when some of the um reason for doing it becomes extrinsic monetary.

key_insights.insight: It's important to regularly check in with yourself and assess your feelings towards your work to ensure you're not becoming someone you didn't intend to be.
key_insights.quote: So that constant asking of that question how do i feel about this today which as you alluded to means turning down money sometimes but you know thinking longer term about what you're doing and why you're doing i think is so critical

key_insights.insight: Confidence to make a career leap can stem from analyzing the potential earnings and lifestyle using a spreadsheet to project future scenarios.
key_insights.quote: So for me, my confidence came from an Excel spreadsheet...I looked at what my long-term potential earnings and lifestyle would look like if I stayed in the banking industry. And then I looked at what my numbers were looking like. You know, if I stayed as a creator, where could I take it?

key_insights.insight: Taking a gradual approach, like a 'toe dip', into a new career by maintaining part-time employment can mitigate risk.
key_insights.quote: I didn't just jump out. I kind of took a toe dip in and I quit my job, but then I took another job that was part-time so I could make content and have a job as well.

key_insights.insight: Success is a personal journey and not a defined destination; the goalpost is always moving.
key_insights.quote: Maybe the goalpost is just moving off into the future. Maybe that speaks to what life is. It's just that journey as opposed to that destination, right?

actionable_advice.advice: Quantify your career risks and rewards by projecting potential future earnings and lifestyle.
actionable_advice.how_to: Create an Excel spreadsheet to compare potential earnings and lifestyle if you stay in your current career versus pursuing your creative endeavors.

actionable_advice.advice: Gradually transition into a new career or passion project.
actionable_advice.how_to: Start by reducing your hours at your current job and dedicate the extra time to your new pursuit, or take a part-time job in your new field.

actionable_advice.advice: Regularly ask yourself 'How do I feel about this?' to ensure you are creating the life you want.
actionable_advice.how_to: Set a recurring reminder (daily/weekly) to consciously reflect on how you feel about the work you're doing and the direction you're heading.

memorable_stories: Patricia didn't tell her parents she quit her banking job until she felt more secure in her YouTube career.
Patricia spent 7 years consistently creating content before reaching a million subscribers.
contrarian_claims: Financial compensation can decrease intrinsic motivation and enjoyment of previously loved activities.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 64 - What You Need To Know About Following Your Passion: Patricia Bright
